hero.name: JBMF — Java BMF Port
hero.welcome_md: The Java port of [BMF](/people/bmf-grammar) — second implementation of the parser, targeting the JVM instead of native C++. Compiles to [Jasmin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jasmin_(software)) JVM assembly. Every BML source file in the archive carries `Compiler: JBMF (R) Compiler` in its header — by the time those files were typed, the system was already bootstrapping itself through Java.
hero.overlay_class: absolute inset-0 bg-gradient-to-t from-background via-background/85 to-background/30

facts.label: Year
facts.value_md: 2000 · ships in the archive at Java/JBMF.exe

facts.label: Substrate
facts.value_md: Java · Jasmin JVM assembly · cross-platform where the JVM runs

facts.label: Sister VM
facts.value_md: [BMCPU](/people/bmcpu-vm) — the C++/COM Win32 host. Same parser; different substrate.

facts.label: Drives
facts.value_md: Compiles [BML](/people/bml-language) source to executable artifacts on the JVM. Header banner on every `.bml` file.

facts.label: Ancestry
facts.value_md: [Jasmin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jasmin_(software)) — the JVM-bytecode assembler that made it tractable to generate verifier-clean class files in 2000.

articles.body_md: BMF's grammar is described in BML; the parser itself can be implemented anywhere there's a host language. The 2000 project shipped two: one in C++ ( [BMCPU](/people/bmcpu-vm) ) targeting Win32 + COM, and one in Java (JBMF) targeting the JVM. The grammar source file `BMF-grammar.bml` was the same for both. The same conviction shows up across the network now. The Coherence Network's API speaks JSON over HTTP; clients are web (TypeScript), CLI (Node + Python wrapper), and direct graph access (Cypher). The interface is the contract; the host substrate is incidental. JBMF was the first time that posture was load-bearing in a system this body shipped.
articles.heading: Two substrates, one parser
JBMF (R) Compiler

metadata.title: JBMF — Java BMF Port (2000)
metadata.description: The substrate-portable second implementation of BMF — a Java port targeting Jasmin JVM bytecode. The JBMF (R) Compiler banner appears at the top of every BML source sample in the archive: the system was already self-bootstrapped through Java by the time those files were written.

en.footer_md: Public archive: [master-thesis-2000/](https://github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network/tree/main/docs/field/urs/artifacts/master-thesis-2000). The JBMF.exe binary itself lives in the larger 139 MB Angelic archive (off-repo, on disk) — see [EXTERNAL.md](https://github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network/blob/main/docs/field/urs/artifacts/master-thesis-2000/EXTERNAL.md) for the cluster map.

note_from_body.body_md: A language with one implementation is a project. A language with two implementations on different substrates is *portable architecture*. JBMF demonstrated that BML's semantics — including the speculation, the choose-and-undo, the four-ancestor synthesis — translated cleanly across substrates. Same parser, different VM. Same source files, same banner, different runtime.
